title: Why the Eiffel Tower Earns Money While You Sleep #Shorts
desc: By day, the Eiffel Tower's image is public domain — free to use since 1993, seventy years after Gustave Eiffel's death. But a French court ruled the tower's nightly light show is a separate copyrighted artwork, so commercial photos of it lit up at night need permission. In 2002 alone, those image fees topped one million euros.
